title: The Eumenides
description: The Eumenides — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Eumenides" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Achilles
memes.description: Achilles was the greatest Greek warrior of the Trojan War, indirectly connected to the Oresteia through the sacrifice of Iphigenia at Aulis.

memes.label: The Acropolis
memes.description: The rocky citadel of Athens, crowned with Athena's temple, where Orestes supplicates at the goddess's ancient image — the destination of his journey from Delphi in The Eumenides

memes.label: Aegisthus
memes.description: Son of Thyestes, lover of Clytemnestra, and murderer (with her) of Agamemnon — whose own death at Orestes' hands is one link in the chain of vengeance that The Eumenides resolves

memes.label: Agamemnon
memes.description: King of Mycenae and commander of the Greek forces at Troy, whose murder by Clytemnestra and whose demand for vengeance through his son Orestes form the backstory of The Eumenides

memes.label: Agamemnon (Character Analysis)
memes.description: A structural analysis of Agamemnon as a character across the Oresteia — absent yet omnipresent in The Eumenides.

memes.label: "The Amazons' Camp"
memes.description: "The fortress camp built by the Amazons on the hill that became the Areopagus during their invasion of Athens — the origin of the hill's name, recounted by Athena in her founding speech"

memes.label: Anthropomorphism in Greek Religion
memes.description: Anthropomorphism — the representation of gods in human form — is a central theme in Murray's analysis of the Oresteia's theological achievement.

memes.label: Apollo
memes.description: The god of prophecy at Delphi, who commands Orestes to avenge Agamemnon, purifies him of the resulting pollution, and serves as his advocate before the Areopagus in The Eumenides

memes.label: Apollo's Arrival at Delphi
memes.description: Apollo's peaceful arrival at Delphi — escorted by the people of Hephaistos and receiving the oracle as a gift from Phoebe — is Aeschylus' revision of traditional myth.

memes.label: Areopagus (Court of Athens)
memes.description: The ancient Athenian council and court — established, according to Aeschylus' The Eumenides, by Athena herself to try Orestes and thereby replace the blood-feud with civic justice

memes.label: Areopagus Hill (Hill of Ares)
memes.description: The rocky hill northwest of the Athenian Acropolis where the Areopagus court met and beneath which the Eumenides received their shrine — the sacred site where divine justice became civic law

memes.label: Ares
memes.description: The Olympian god of war, whose mythological association with the Areopagus (the "Hill of Ares") gives the Athenian homicide court its name and sacred authority in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: Argos (Mycenae)
memes.description: The kingdom of the House of Atreus — Agamemnon's realm and Orestes' homeland — from which the cycle of vengeance originates and to which Orestes returns after his acquittal

memes.label: Artemis
memes.description: Artemis is the goddess of the hunt, wild animals, and childbirth, whose anger at Agamemnon set the Oresteia's chain of vengeance in motion.

memes.label: Athena
memes.description: The goddess of wisdom, daughter of Zeus alone, who in The Eumenides founds the Areopagus court, casts the deciding vote for Orestes' acquittal, and persuades the Furies to become the Eumenides

memes.label: "Athena Persuades the Furies"
memes.description: "Athena's rhetorical and diplomatic persuasion of the defeated Erinyes to accept a new home and role as benevolent guardians (Eumenides) in Athens — the key act of reconciliation that transforms the play's theological arc from vengeance to civic harmony"

memes.label: Chorus of Athenian Citizens
memes.description: The jury of twelve Athenian citizens empaneled by Athena to hear the case of Orestes — the first jury in the first homicide court, whose silent presence represents the birth of civic justice

memes.label: Athens
memes.description: The city of Athena, where the Areopagus court is founded and the Furies are transformed into the Eumenides in the culminating action of Aeschylus' play

memes.label: Aulis
memes.description: Aulis was the harbor where the Greek fleet gathered before sailing to Troy, and the site where Agamemnon sacrificed Iphigenia to appease Artemis.

memes.label: Blood-Pollution (Miasma)
memes.description: The Greek concept of ritual pollution (miasma) caused by kindred bloodshed — the stain that falls upon the killer and all who associate with them in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: Calchas
memes.description: Calchas was the Greek seer who interpreted Artemis' demand for the sacrifice of Iphigenia at Aulis, setting the Oresteia's tragic chain in motion.

memes.label: Cassandra
memes.description: Cassandra was the Trojan prophetess, daughter of Priam, brought to Argos as Agamemnon's concubine and murdered alongside him by Clytemnestra.

memes.label: Chalcidice
memes.description: Chalcidice is the peninsula in northern Greece containing Pallene, traditionally identified as the site of the Gigantomachy.

memes.label: Chthonian (Underworld Divinity)
memes.description: The Greek religious concept of the chthonian — belonging to the earth, underworld, or the dead — characterizing the Erinyes, the ancestral dead, and Mother Earth as sources of ancient authority in The Eumenides

memes.label: Civic Order
memes.description: The political concept of civic order — the rule of law administered by citizen institutions — that Aeschylus' The Eumenides presents as the culmination of divine justice through the founding of the Areopagus

memes.label: Clytemnestra
memes.description: The murdered queen of Mycenae, whose ghost appears in The Eumenides to rouse the sleeping Furies and demand vengeance against her son Orestes for the matricide

memes.label: "The Appearance of Clytemnestra's Ghost"
memes.description: "The ghost of Clytemnestra appearing to the sleeping Furies at Delphi, goading them to resume their pursuit of Orestes — a pivotal dramatic reversal in the opening scenes of The Eumenides"

memes.label: Codrus
memes.description: Codrus was the legendary last king of Athens who sacrificed himself to save the city — cited by Murray as an example of the sacrificial logic rejected by Aeschylus.

memes.label: "Corycian Cave"
memes.description: "The Corycian Cave on Mount Parnassus above Delphi — a sacred grotto of the Nymphs and Pan, mentioned in the Pythian Prophetess's opening prayer as part of Delphi's sacred landscape"

memes.label: Curse (Ara)
memes.description: The curse (Greek ἀρά, ara) in Greek thought is the active principle by which the Erinyes operate — the wrath of the wronged dead manifesting as blight, barrenness, plague, and the relentless pursuit of the transgressor.

memes.label: The Death of Agamemnon
memes.description: The murder of Agamemnon by Clytemnestra and Aegisthus upon his return from Troy is the central event preceding the action of The Eumenides.

memes.label: The Delian Seas
memes.description: The Delian seas are the waters around the island of Delos, Apollo's birthplace, referenced by the Pythia in the opening speech of The Eumenides.

memes.label: Delos
memes.description: Apollo's birthplace island in the Aegean Sea, referenced in the Prophetess's opening prayer at Delphi.

memes.label: Delphi
memes.description: The ancient Panhellenic sanctuary of Apollo on Mount Parnassus — the site of the Delphic Oracle, the scene of Orestes' purification from blood-guilt in The Eumenides, and the first stop on his journey toward civic justice in Athens

memes.label: Delphic Oracle
memes.description: The prophetic shrine of Apollo at Delphi — the most important oracle in the Greek world, where Orestes first seeks purification and whose theological history is recounted in the opening speech of The Eumenides

memes.label: Delphos
memes.description: The eponymous king of Delphi, mentioned in the Pythian Prophetess's opening prayer in The Eumenides — the mortal ruler who welcomed Apollo and whose people "glorified his name"

memes.label: Dionysus
memes.description: Dionysus (Bromios) is the god of wine, ecstasy, and ritual madness, whose Maenadic rites were practiced on Mount Parnassus near the Corycian Cave.

memes.label: Divine Succession
memes.description: Divine succession — the orderly transfer of power from older to younger gods — is a central theological theme in The Eumenides, modeled through the peaceful Delphic succession.

memes.label: "Dramatis Personae"
memes.description: "Cast of characters in Aeschylus' The Eumenides"

memes.label: Electra
memes.description: Daughter of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, sister of Orestes — a central figure in other versions of the Orestes myth (especially Sophocles and Euripides) but not appearing in The Eumenides

memes.label: "Endnotes"
memes.description: "Endnotes and scholarly commentary for The Eumenides"

memes.label: Ephialtes
memes.description: The Athenian democratic reformer whose curtailment of the Areopagus' political powers in 462 BCE forms the political backdrop to Aeschylus' The Eumenides, which defends the court's traditional religious authority

memes.label: Epieikeia (Equity)
memes.description: Epieikeia (Greek ἐπιείκεια) is the Aristotelian concept of equity — the correction of law where it fails owing to its generality — which Aeschylus anticipates in the Eumenides through Athena's founding of a court capable of understanding the particular circumstances of each case.

memes.label: Erechtheus
memes.description: Erechtheus was a legendary king of Athens who sacrificed his daughters for the city — an act cited by Murray as belonging to the primitive world superseded by Aeschylus' Zeus.

memes.label: Erichthonius
memes.description: Erichthonius was a legendary king of Athens, born from the earth and Hephaistos, representing Athenian autochthony and the city's special relationship with Athena.

memes.label: The Erinyes (Furies)
memes.description: The ancient chthonian goddesses of vengeance — the chorus of Aeschylus' The Eumenides — who pursue Orestes for matricide, are defeated at the trial, and are persuaded by Athena to become the Eumenides, guardians of Athens

memes.label: The Founding of the Areopagus
memes.description: The establishment by Athena of the Areopagus court on the Hill of Ares in Athens — the founding act of civic justice that transforms the blood-feud into deliberative law

memes.label: Gender and Authority in the Trial of Orestes
memes.description: An analysis of the gendered arguments in the trial of Orestes, where Apollo's claim that the mother is not a true parent — and Athena's unique position as a motherless goddess — determine the outcome of the first homicide trial.

memes.label: Helen of Troy
memes.description: Helen was the queen of Sparta and sister of Clytemnestra, whose abduction by Paris triggered the Trojan War.

memes.label: Hephaistos
memes.description: Hephaistos is the god of fire and metalworking, mentioned in The Eumenides as the ancestor of the people who escorted Apollo to Delphi.

memes.label: Hera
memes.description: Queen of the Olympian gods and goddess of marriage, invoked in The Eumenides by Apollo in his argument before the Areopagus that the marriage bond sanctified by Hera and Zeus outweighs the claims of maternal kinship

memes.label: Hermes
memes.description: The messenger god and guide of travelers, commissioned by Apollo in The Eumenides to shepherd Orestes on his journey from Delphi to Athens

memes.label: Hubris (Overstepping / Arrogance)
memes.description: The Greek concept of overstepping one's allotted portion (Moira) — the act of arrogant transgression that triggers the retributive law of Dikê in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: "Introduction"
memes.description: "Introduction to Aeschylus' The Eumenides — background, themes, and the Greek concept of justice, the blood-feud, and divine law"

memes.label: Io
memes.description: Io was a mortal woman loved by Zeus and persecuted by Hera, whose eventual redemption is cited by Murray as a parallel to Orestes' story.

memes.label: Iphigenia
memes.description: Daughter of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, sacrificed by her father at Aulis to obtain favorable winds for the Trojan fleet — the original wrong that sets the chain of vengeance in the Oresteia in motion

memes.label: Ixion
memes.description: Ixion was a mythological king who murdered a kinsman, was purified by Zeus, and became the archetypal forgiven sinner in Murray's analysis of The Eumenides.

memes.label: Dikê (Justice)
memes.description: The Greek concept of retributive justice as a natural law — "the doer must suffer" — and its transformation in Aeschylus' The Eumenides through the will of Zeus

memes.label: "Justice in The Eumenides: From Vendetta to Civic Order"
memes.description: A synthetic analysis of how Aeschylus' The Eumenides traces the transformation of justice from the mechanical blood-feud enforced by the Furies to the deliberative civic justice of Athena's Areopagus court.

memes.label: Kindred Blood
memes.description: Kindred blood is the blood shared by members of the same family — its shedding constitutes the gravest form of pollution in Greek religion.

memes.label: Lake Tritonis
memes.description: Mythical lake in Libya, traditionally identified as Athena's birthplace, referenced in Orestes' prayer.

memes.label: Learning by Suffering (Pathei Mathos)
memes.description: The Aeschylean doctrine that Zeus "established Learning by Suffering to be an abiding law" — the theological principle that suffering teaches wisdom and enables divine forgiveness in The Eumenides

memes.label: Leda
memes.description: Leda was the queen of Sparta, mother of Clytemnestra and Helen, connected to the mythological background of the Oresteia.

memes.label: Leto
memes.description: Leto is the Titaness mother of Apollo and Artemis, and the daughter of Phoebe, connecting the Delphic oracle to the genealogical chain of the Titans.

memes.label: Loxias (Apollo as Prophet)
memes.description: Loxias is a cultic title of Apollo at Delphi emphasizing his role as the interpreter of Zeus's will through oracle and prophecy.

memes.label: Menelaus
memes.description: Menelaus was the king of Sparta, brother of Agamemnon, and husband of Helen, whose war against Troy is the background of the Oresteia.

memes.label: Moira (Fate / Allotted Portion)
memes.description: The Greek concept of each being's allotted portion — the boundary that must not be exceeded — a foundational element of Dikê in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: Mother Earth (Gaia)
memes.description: Gaia (Mother Earth) as a cosmic principle in Aeschylus' The Eumenides — the supreme chthonian authority who executes the law of Dikê, is polluted by kindred bloodshed, and whose prophetic shrine at Delphi passes to Apollo

memes.label: Mount Parnassus
memes.description: The sacred mountain rising above Delphi, home to the Corycian Nymphs, the Muses, and Dionysus — the physical setting for Apollo's oracle in The Eumenides

memes.label: Oath (Horkos)
memes.description: The oath (Greek ὅρκος, horkos) was the central mechanism of primitive Greek justice — a sworn declaration presumed to settle all disputes — which Athena supersedes in the Eumenides by founding a court of inquiry capable of examining the full circumstances of a case.

memes.label: The Olympian-Chthonian Conflict in The Eumenides
memes.description: An analysis of the theological conflict in Aeschylus' The Eumenides between the older chthonian powers (the Furies, Night, Fate) and the younger Olympian gods (Apollo, Athena, Zeus), and how their reconciliation founds Athenian civic order.
aliases: The Old Gods and the New

memes.label: Olympian Order
memes.description: The new divine dispensation under Zeus and the Olympian gods — characterized by understanding, forgiveness, and the rule of law — that supersedes the chthonian order of mechanical retribution in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: The Omphalos (Navel-Stone)
memes.description: The Omphalos is the sacred stone at Delphi believed to mark the center (navel) of the earth, where Orestes seeks refuge in The Eumenides.

memes.label: Orestes
memes.description: Son of Agamemnon and Clytemnestra, the protagonist of Aeschylus' The Eumenides — a matricide pursued by the Furies who seeks purification at Delphi and justice at Athens

memes.label: Pallene
memes.description: Pallene was the peninsula in Chalcidice traditionally identified as the location of Phlegra, the battlefield of the Gigantomachy.

memes.label: Pan
memes.description: Pan is the god of the wild, shepherds, and rustic music, worshipped alongside the Nymphs at the Corycian Cave on Mount Parnassus.

memes.label: Paris of Troy
memes.description: Paris was the prince of Troy whose abduction of Helen triggered the Trojan War, the essential background of the Oresteia.

memes.label: Peitho (Persuasion)
memes.description: Peitho (Persuasion) is the goddess and principle of persuasive speech, central to Athena's strategy for resolving the conflict with the Furies in The Eumenides.

memes.label: Pentheus
memes.description: Pentheus was the king of Thebes who was torn apart by Maenads — a story referenced by the Pythia in her opening prayer in The Eumenides.

memes.label: Pergamon
memes.description: Pergamon was an ancient Greek city in Asia Minor, famous for its Great Altar depicting the Gigantomachy — the battle of the Olympian gods against the Giants.

memes.label: Phlegra
memes.description: Mythical site of the Gigantomachy (Battle of Gods and Giants), referenced in Orestes' prayer to Athena.

memes.label: Phocis
memes.description: Phocis is the region of central Greece that contains Delphi and Mount Parnassus, the sacred landscape of The Eumenides.

memes.label: Phoebe
memes.description: Phoebe is a Titaness in Greek mythology who held the Delphic oracle before passing it peacefully to her grandson Apollo.

memes.label: Pleistos River
memes.description: The River Pleistos near Delphi, mentioned in the Pythian Prophetess's opening prayer as part of Delphi's sacred landscape in Aeschylus' Eumenides.

memes.label: Poseidon
memes.description: Poseidon is the god of the sea, earthquakes, and horses, invoked by the Pythian Prophetess alongside the Pleistos River at Delphi.

memes.label: Prometheus
memes.description: Prometheus is the Titan who gave fire to humanity, central to Aeschylus' Prometheus-trilogy which develops the theme of Zeus as a learning god.

memes.label: Prophecy in Greek Religion
memes.description: Prophecy (manteia) was a central institution of Greek religion, with Delphi as its supreme seat — as dramatized in The Eumenides' opening scene.

memes.label: Purification (Katharmos)
memes.description: The Greek religious ritual of cleansing (katharmos) from blood-pollution — the act performed by Apollo for Orestes that enables the transition from fugitive to suppliant in The Eumenides

memes.label: The Purification at Delphi
memes.description: The ritual cleansing of Orestes by Apollo at the Delphic shrine — the act that transforms the matricide from a polluted fugitive into a suppliant entitled to seek justice at Athens

memes.label: The Pythian Games
memes.description: The Pythian Games were one of the four Panhellenic festivals, held at Delphi in honor of Apollo's victory over the Python.

memes.label: The Pythian Prophetess
memes.description: The priestess of Apollo at Delphi, whose opening speech and terrified discovery of Orestes at the altar launches the action of The Eumenides

memes.label: Python
memes.description: Python was the great serpent or dragon that guarded Delphi before Apollo, according to the traditional myth that Aeschylus deliberately revises.

memes.label: Reconciliation and Forgiveness
memes.description: Reconciliation and forgiveness in Aeschylus' Eumenides represent the theological breakthrough by which the mechanical law of retribution is transformed — not abolished — through the will of Zeus, the persuasion of Athena, and the incorporation of the Furies into the civic order.

memes.label: The Sacrifice of Iphigenia
memes.description: The sacrifice of Iphigenia at Aulis by her father Agamemnon to appease Artemis is the originating crime of the Oresteia's cycle of vengeance.

memes.label: "Scamander River"
memes.description: "The river near Troy from which Athena returns at her entrance in The Eumenides — a geographic link between the Trojan War and the Athenian trial of Orestes"

memes.label: Sparta
memes.description: Sparta was the city of Menelaus and the homeland of Clytemnestra, whose royal lineage connects the House of Atreus to the Spartan throne.

memes.label: Supplication (Hikesia)
memes.description: The ancient Greek religious institution of supplication (hikesia) — the right of a person in need to seek refuge at a god's altar and receive protection — central to the plot of Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: The Supplication at Athens
memes.description: Orestes' supplication at the statue of Athena on the Athenian Acropolis — the ritual act that invokes the goddess's protection and initiates the trial before the Areopagus

memes.label: Tauris
memes.description: Tauris was the land to which Artemis transported Iphigenia in some versions of the myth, where she served as priestess of the Tauric Artemis.

memes.label: The Areopagus Court
memes.description: The ancient Athenian council and homicide court, founded according to The Eumenides by Athena to try Orestes — the institution that transforms the blood-feud into civic justice

memes.label: Athena's Areopagus Speech
memes.description: Athena's founding speech for the Areopagus is the constitutional moment of The Eumenides — the performative utterance that establishes the court and defines civic justice.

memes.label: The Athenian Jury
memes.description: The Athenian jury of twelve citizens, selected by Athena to judge Orestes, represents the democratic principle of judgment by one's peers.

memes.label: The Binding Song of the Furies
memes.description: The Binding Song is the ritual chant by which the Furies attempt to bind Orestes, a dramatic climax of choral power in The Eumenides.

memes.label: The Blood-Feud
memes.description: The primitive Greek institution of kin-vengeance — the obligation of a murdered person's relatives to avenge the death — the system that Aeschylus' The Eumenides depicts as superseded by the Areopagus court

memes.label: The Chthonian People
memes.description: The Chthonian people are the dead who dwell beneath the earth, whose wrath against the living is expressed through the Furies in The Eumenides.

memes.label: "The Council of the Areopagus"
memes.description: "The council of twelve Athenian citizens empaneled by Athena as the first homicide court jury in The Eumenides — the human institution through which divine justice becomes civic deliberation"

memes.label: The Delphic Priesthood
memes.description: The prophetic institution at Delphi, staffed by the Pythia and temple priests, through which Apollo — as the Prophêtês Dios — reveals the will of Zeus to humanity

memes.label: The Departure from Delphi
memes.description: The departure of Orestes from Delphi under Hermes' escort marks the transition from divine sanctuary to civic justice in the action of The Eumenides.

memes.label: The Eagles' Omen
memes.description: The omen of two eagles devouring a pregnant hare, interpreted by Calchas at Aulis, is the prophetic event that sets the Oresteia's chain of vengeance in motion.

memes.label: The Ephetae
memes.description: The Ephetae were a body of fifty-one Athenian judges associated with the Areopagus Court, traditionally connected to homicide trials.

memes.label: "The Eumenides"
memes.description: "The complete text of Aeschylus' The Eumenides, translated by Gilbert Murray"

memes.label: "The Chorus of Furies"
memes.description: "The Chorus of the Furies (Erinyes) — the collective dramatic voice of the ancient law in Aeschylus' The Eumenides, performing the Binding Song, the dance of pursuit, and ultimately the blessing of Athens"

memes.label: The Gigantomachy
memes.description: The Gigantomachy was the cosmic battle in which the Olympian gods defeated the Giants, frequently referenced in The Eumenides.

memes.label: The House of Atreus
memes.description: The doomed royal dynasty of Mycenae/Argos — the family of Agamemnon, Clytemnestra, and Orestes — whose multigenerational cycle of crime and vengeance forms the backstory of Aeschylus' Oresteia

memes.label: The Law of the Moirai
memes.description: The Law of the Moirai is the mechanical, impersonal operation of justice-as-retribution that The Eumenides seeks to transcend through the will of Zeus.

memes.label: The Nymphs of Parnassus
memes.description: The Nymphs are nature spirits who inhabit the Corycian Cave on Mount Parnassus, acknowledged by the Pythia in her opening prayer.

memes.label: The Olympian Gods
memes.description: The divine order under Zeus — the "younger gods" of The Eumenides, including Apollo, Athena, and Hermes — whose capacity to learn, forgive, and establish institutions supersedes the mechanical law of the chthonian powers

memes.label: The Oresteia
memes.description: The Oresteia is Aeschylus' trilogy of tragedies, of which The Eumenides is the concluding play.

memes.label: The Polis (City-State)
memes.description: The polis is the Greek city-state — the political community whose health and order The Eumenides celebrates as the ultimate achievement of justice.

memes.label: The Suppliant Tradition
memes.description: The tradition of the suppliant (hiketês) is central to ancient Greek religion and law — the right of the vulnerable to seek divine and human protection.

memes.label: The Suppliant Ritual
memes.description: The suppliant ritual (hikesia) involved specific tokens — an olive branch wound with white wool — and sacred gestures that Orestes performs in The Eumenides.

memes.label: The Throne of Delphi
memes.description: The Throne of Delphi is the seat of prophecy, held successively by Earth, Themis, Phoebe, and Apollo — a peaceful succession that models the play's resolution.

memes.label: The Titanomachy
memes.description: The Titanomachy was the ten-year war between the Olympian gods and the Titans, establishing Zeus's cosmic order as the background to the Oresteia.

memes.label: The Trial of Orestes
memes.description: The trial of Orestes before the Areopagus Court is the climactic event of The Eumenides, representing the transition from blood-vengeance to civic justice.

memes.label: The Trojan War
memes.description: The Trojan War was the ten-year conflict between the Greeks and Troy, forming the historical and mythological background of the Oresteia.

memes.label: Themis (Divine Custom)
memes.description: The Greek concept of correct tribal custom — "the thing that is always done" — one of the foundational forces underlying Dikê in Aeschylus' The Eumenides

memes.label: Theseus
memes.description: The legendary king of Athens, whose sons, according to Athena's speech in The Eumenides, gave her land near the Scamander river — establishing the Athenian connection that underlies the play's civic theology

memes.label: Thyestes
memes.description: Thyestes was the brother of Atreus and father of Aegisthus, whose curse upon the House of Atreus is the ancestral background of The Eumenides.

memes.label: The Transformation of the Furies
memes.description: The persuasion of the Erinyes by Athena to abandon their destructive wrath and become the Eumenides ("Kindly Ones") — the culminating transformation of The Eumenides in which the old law is reconciled with the new

memes.label: The Trial of Orestes
memes.description: The founding trial of the Areopagus court in Athens — presided over by Athena, with Apollo as advocate and the Furies as prosecutors — at which Orestes is acquitted of matricide and the blood-feud is superseded by civic justice

memes.label: Troy
memes.description: Ancient city of Troy (Ilium), site of the Trojan War, referenced in the Eumenides as the theater of Agamemnon's victory and the origin of Athena's Athenian land grant.

memes.label: Tyndareus
memes.description: Tyndareus was the king of Sparta, father of Clytemnestra and Helen, whose royal lineage is the background of the Oresteia.

memes.label: The Waking of the Furies
memes.description: The appearance of Clytemnestra's ghost at Delphi, who rouses the sleeping Furies from their exhausted slumber and demands that they resume their pursuit of Orestes

memes.label: Zeus
memes.description: The supreme Olympian god, who in Aeschylus' theology is the divine Father who "made a road to Thought" and whose capacity to learn and forgive enables the resolution of The Eumenides — though he never appears on stage
